scope.summary: R-fail adds a rig lane for deterministic local failure injection over the installed artifact, plus committed evidence from a full pass.
out_of_scope: New product-only fault-injection hooks for commit_in_doubt or confirmation-grant TTL manipulation.
Disruptive Docker container restart inside the default shared-checkout lane.

entry_points.name: bash scripts/rig/rig.sh failure-injection --log
entry_points.trace: rig.sh dispatches to scripts/rig/failure_injection_lanes.sh, which runs the committed installed-artifact harness.

entry_points.name: bash scripts/rig/failure_injection_lanes.sh run --log
entry_points.trace: Full pass recorded in tests/artifacts/evidence/rfail-failure-injection-lanes.json with source_sha 4e7683a2a46d4c5d0e8fba25efe25b9b8a7cb1e5.

entry_points.name: scripts/swarm_discipline.sh bounded-run --timeout 1800 -- bash scripts/rig/failure_injection_lanes.sh run --log
entry_points.trace: Exited 0. The installed-artifact lane passed wire_client_revoke, wire_client_rotate, wire_server_restart, and wire_oauth_token_expiry; it also ran the D10 idle-kill subprocess assertion successfully.

entry_points.name: R-fail syntax and whitespace checks
entry_points.trace: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m py_compile scripts/rig/failure_injection_lanes.py, bash -n scripts/rig/failure_injection_lanes.sh, and git diff --check over the R-fail harness paths all exited 0.

limitations: The D10 idle-kill lane is included as a supplemental subprocess assertion because the public installed artifact redacts SID/SERIAL and exposes no deterministic wire kill hook.
The lane proves server process restart, not a disruptive Docker container restart, in the default shared-checkout run.
R-fail does not by itself unblock E5's grant-expiry timing or commit_in_doubt injection gaps.
follow_ups: Use the R-fail revoke, restart, OAuth expiry, rotation, and D10 idle-kill mechanisms from E5; file separate hooks for E5's grant-expiry and commit_in_doubt injection gaps if they remain required.
